ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Release year of the movie */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the movie */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie poster */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie trailer */,
  "imdb_rating" REAL /* IMDB rating of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., Adventure, Animation, Children) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing the rating */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (1-5) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the rating */,
  "review" TEXT /* Optional review text provided by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rating_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical rating */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the rating */,
  "review" TEXT /* Optional review text provided by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rating_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rating_history_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recommendations" (
  "rec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recommendation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recommended movie */,
  "rec_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recommendation */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the recommendation (e.g., similar movies, user preferences) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "search_history" (
  "search_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each search */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "search_query" TEXT /* Search query entered by the user */,
  "search_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the search */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("search_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_search_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_stats" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "avg_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the movie */,
  "total_ratings" INTEGER /* Total number of ratings for the movie */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Total views of the movie */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Engagement score (e.g., likes, comments, shares) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "preference_type" TEXT /* Type of preference (e.g., genre, movie, actor) */,
  "preference_value" TEXT /* Value of the preference (e.g., Action, Toy Story, Tom Hanks) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "preference_type", "preference_value")
)
